key_insight: The paper shows that bilevel optimization can be solved to second-order stationarity with an optimal deterministic \(\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^{-1.5})\) rate using a single-loop cubic-regularized Newton strategy.
engineering_relevance: This enables more efficient hyperparameter, meta-learning, and design optimization pipelines where the upper-level objective is nonconvex but the lower-level problem is strongly convex.
method: Builds a double-loop CRN baseline that attains the optimal outer rate but repeatedly solves the lower-level problem.
Introduces a single-loop cubic regularized Newton method combining one lower-level gradient step with one Newton step for the hypergradient.
Proves deterministic \(\mathcal{O}(\varepsilon^{-1.5})\) total oracle complexity for finding an \(\varepsilon\)-SOSP of the hyperfunction.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical convergence and oracle-complexity guarantees only; no numerical experiments or datasets are mentioned.
watch_item: The result depends on accurate hypergradient and hyper-Hessian control, and the abstract notes that seemingly simple method variants may lose convergence.
paper_title: On Second-Order Methods for Bilevel Optimization
